description: Pediatric airway emergency (croup with stridor + respiratory distress) in a 5-year-old.

patient.name: Emma L., 5F
patient.chief_complaint: Barking cough, stridor at rest, labored breathing with accessory muscle use, retractions. Woke from sleep unable to breathe normally. Parent reports symptoms worsened rapidly over 2 hours.
patient.history: No significant PMH, vaccinations up to date

rubric_notes: Pediatric airway emergency. Stridor AT REST + accessory muscle use + retractions = impending airway failure. Multiple critical vitals (HR >130 critical even for age-adjusted pediatric, RR >30 critical, SpO2 <94 concerning, temp >39.5 critical, GCS <15 concerning). Per RUBRIC §3 airway emergency = ESI 1. Pediatric vitals adjusted but still trip the rules. Croup or epiglottitis; nebulized epinephrine + dexamethasone immediate; do NOT examine throat without secured airway.
